description_long: This is a cylindrical, off-white wax candle with a slightly irregular, melted top surface and a visible brown wick protruding from the center. The candle's body shows subtle undulations and asymmetry, suggesting it has been partially melted or used, with one side lower and more eroded than the other. The surface is matte and smooth, with some visible wax drips and softened edges, giving it a realistic, hand-poured appearance. There is no visible holder or base, and the candle stands upright on its own, with a flat bottom for stability. The overall color is a creamy white, with the wick providing a contrasting brown accent. This type of candle is commonly found as a decorative element on tables, window sills, or outdoor urban settings such as patios and parks.
description: A partially melted, off-white cylindrical wax candle with a visible brown wick and subtle wax drips. The surface is matte and smooth, with an irregular, softened top edge.
description_view_0: The candle is seen from a slightly angled front, showing the irregular, melted top and a prominent brown wick, with visible wax drips on the left side.
description_view_1: The candle is viewed from the back, displaying a smoother, more uniform cylindrical surface with minimal visible wax drips.
description_view_2: This side view highlights the uneven, melted top edge and the lower, more eroded side of the candle, with the wick still visible.
description_view_3: The candle is seen from a rear angle, emphasizing the asymmetry of the melted top and the subtle undulations along the body.
